inlineContent.text: is the fundamental type providing the common attributes and
methods for all object types in GTK, Pango and other libraries
based on GObject. The
class provides methods for object
construction and destruction, property access methods, and signal
support. Signals are described in detail
For a tutorial on implementing a new
class, see
For a list of naming conventions for GObjects and their methods, see the
. For the high-level concepts
behind GObject, read
Since GLib 2.72, all
s are guaranteed to be aligned to at least the
alignment of the largest basic GLib type (typically this is
or
). If you need larger alignment for an element in a
, you
should allocate it on the heap (aligned), or arrange for your
to be
appropriately padded. This guarantee applies to the
(or derived)
struct, the
(or derived) struct, and any private data allocated
by
The
protocol exposes the methods and properties of an underlying
instance.
The default implementation of these can be found in the protocol extension below.
For a concrete class that implements these methods and properties, see
Alternatively, use
as a lighweight,
reference if you already have an instance you just want to use.
